title: Whats up with that
wikitext:
[]: Whats up with that? {Spoilers OH NO!}             Quote Post by UltraBlackPilot » Wed Oct 28, 2009 6:47 pm So I Need something cleared up. When 01 was killing 03, whats up with the choice of song? I mean, maybe the lyrics mean something but I was watching it feeling completely mellow even though the screen showed something getting torn limb from limb. is this just an Evangelion sorta thing or is there some sort of deeper meaning to it?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Oct 28, 2009 6:56 pm That is one of my favorite scenes in 2.0 because it reminded me of Kom Susser Todd from EoE. Well if you look at the translated lyrics of the song it'll shed some light I guess. I'd have to check it out again.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Wed Oct 28, 2009 7:20 pm The song that plays reflects on how Asuka is Shinji's friend and how's she's getting torn to shreds.

[]:             Quote Post by Mongoosedog » Wed Oct 28, 2009 7:39 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: That is one of my favorite scenes in 2.0 because it reminded me of Kom Susser Tod in EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by UltraBlackPilot » Wed Oct 28, 2009 8:15 pm Eva 04 wrote: The song that plays reflects on how Asuka is Shinji's friend and how's she's getting torn to shreds.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Oct 29, 2009 7:10 am UltraBlackPilot wrote: That doesn't make sense to me, when is Asuka anybodys friend?

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Thu Oct 29, 2009 7:21 am SaltyJoe wrote: Well, the scene where they spend some time together on the same futon supposedly establishes this: i'm maybe wrong, but as far as i know, being permitted to address someone by their first name is a much bigger thing in Japanese culture than it is in the West, especially if that someone is of higher social standing than the one who is given permission (Asuka is a ranking officer of the Euro Airforce, while Shinji is just random pilot guy in this regard).

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Thu Oct 29, 2009 9:25 am I think scenes like talking on the futon and bickering in class are there to hint at a friendship that takes place mostly off stage. They don't have to share the same frame for 30 minutes to get the point across. Still, this plot point could've been developed a lot better, imo.

[]:             Quote Post by roblucci01 » Thu Oct 29, 2009 12:41 pm The way they used that song is based on a concept of "Opposites" frequently used in modern film-making these days. For instance, the use of the very subtle and relaxing "Unforgettable" by Nat King Cole in the opening death scene of Watchmen. It creates a somewhat depressing and even a very chilling vibe among fans who simply read the death in the comic book, without any music at all. Imagine comparing the manga Unit 03 sequence to Rebuilds take on the scene and describe which version of the scene was more emotional. In other words, film makers do this for dramatic effect, as it gives a sense of irony in using a happy song to something that shouldn't be happy at all. Needless to say, a prime example of this EVERYONE here knows about would be the Komm Susser Todd scene in End of Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Oct 29, 2009 1:16 pm Evangelion has been doing Musical Dissonance since, like...episode three of the original anime. Why the hell is anyone surprised?

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Thu Oct 29, 2009 1:40 pm Are you kidding, nothing says death like a tune like that!

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Thu Oct 29, 2009 2:00 pm Mongoosedog wrote: Joseph the PRPD wrote: That is one of my favorite scenes in 2.0 because it reminded me of Kom Susser Todd from EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by UltraBlackPilot » Fri Oct 30, 2009 12:10 am AuraTwilight wrote: Evangelion has been doing Musical Dissonance since, like...episode three of the original anime. Why the hell is anyone surprised?

[]: Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Oct 30, 2009 12:47 am UltraBlackPilot wrote: AuraTwilight wrote: Evangelion has been doing Musical Dissonance since, like...episode three of the original anime. Why the hell is anyone surprised?

[]: Re: Bl             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Oct 30, 2009 3:07 am Alaska Slim wrote: UltraBlackPilot wrote: AuraTwilight wrote: Evangelion has been doing Musical Dissonance since, like...episode three of the original anime. Why the hell is anyone surprised?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Oct 30, 2009 4:15 am ....Wow, you people.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Oct 30, 2009 4:56 am AuraTwilight wrote: ....Wow, you people.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Oct 30, 2009 1:22 pm Doublechecking, it was episode 4, so I was one off. The point remains the same, though, that musical dissonance has been present since nearly the beginning.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Oct 30, 2009 3:12 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Doublechecking, it was episode 4, so I was one off. The point remains the same, though, that musical dissonance has been present since nearly the beginning.

[]:             Quote Post by UltraBlackPilot » Fri Oct 30, 2009 4:15 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Doublechecking, it was episode 4, so I was one off. The point remains the same, though, that musical dissonance has been present since nearly the beginning.

I mean, maybe the lyrics mean something but I was watching it feeling completely mellow even though the screen showed something getting torn limb from limb. is this just an Evangelion sorta thing or is there some sort of deeper meaning to it?
